rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the one substantive ground-truth concern (yenuo26's point that the reduce_scatter test only checks hasattr, not real parameter behavior), so recall is near-zero for both, though X's repeated flags about missing edge-case test coverage are at least thematically adjacent while Y explicitly rubber-stamps 'Test Coverage: PASS'. Both candidates' findings are grounded and non-fabricated, but X's five nits each name a concrete file/line and a specific concrete fix (e.g. exact test to add), whereas Y's review is mostly narrative verification with only one actionable suggestion phrased vaguely ('worth a one-line comment'). X's verdict (COMMENT) is arguably overcautious for nit-level findings, but its concrete, line-anchored suggestions edge out Y on actionability.
